CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-26T01:03:57.0738767Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-26T01:03:38.329898084Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57489,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] ARM resource common properties should be emitted before other properties\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator emits ARM resource properties in a different order than the old reflection-based generator. Specifically, `properties` is emitted before `name` and `location`, which changes the Bicep output ordering.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe old reflection-based generator sorted properties with this ordering logic (from `Specification.Analyze.cs`):\\n```csharp\\nresource.Properties.OrderBy(p =\\u0026gt; (p.Name == \\u0026#34;Name\\u0026#34; ? 0 : p.IsReadOnly ? \\u0026#39;3\\u0026#39; : p.IsRequired ? \\u0026#39;1\\u0026#39; : \\u0026#39;2\\u0026#39;) + p.Name)\\n```\\n\\nThis ensured `name` was always first, followed by required properties alphabetically, then read-only/output properties.\\n\\nGenerated `DefineProvisionableProperties()`:\\n```csharp\\n_name = DefineProperty(\\u0026#34;Name\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty(\\u0026#34;Location\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;location\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty(\\u0026#34;Properties\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe new generator emits:\\n```csharp\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty(nameof(Properties), new string[] { \\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n_name = DefineProperty(nameof(Name), new string[] { \\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty(nameof(Location), new string[] { \\u0026#34;location\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\nSince the Bicep emitter uses insertion order, this causes `properties:` to appear before `name:` in the Bicep output.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nAll regenerated provisioning libraries will have a different Bicep property ordering than before. While functionally equivalent, this is a behavioral change that breaks existing tests and differs from standard Bicep conventions where `name` and `location` typically appear before `properties`.\\n\\n### Proposed fix\\n\\nThe provisioning generator should ensure ARM resource common properties (`name`, `location`, `tags`) are always defined before the `properties` bag and other custom properties in `DefineProvisionableProperties()`.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-26T01:03:57.0750293Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-26T01:03:38.34366534Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57489,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-26T01:03:57.0774446Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-26T01:03:38.731864974Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57489,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] ARM resource common properties should be emitted before other properties\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator emits ARM resource properties in a different order than the old reflection-based generator. Specifically, `properties` is emitted before `name` and `location`, which changes the Bicep output ordering.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe old reflection-based generator sorted properties with this ordering logic (from `Specification.Analyze.cs`):\\n```csharp\\nresource.Properties.OrderBy(p =\\u0026gt; (p.Name == \\u0026#34;Name\\u0026#34; ? 0 : p.IsReadOnly ? \\u0026#39;3\\u0026#39; : p.IsRequired ? \\u0026#39;1\\u0026#39; : \\u0026#39;2\\u0026#39;) + p.Name)\\n```\\n\\nThis ensured `name` was always first, followed by required properties alphabetically, then read-only/output properties.\\n\\nGenerated `DefineProvisionableProperties()`:\\n```csharp\\n_name = DefineProperty(\\u0026#34;Name\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty(\\u0026#34;Location\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;location\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty(\\u0026#34;Properties\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;], isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe new generator emits:\\n```csharp\\n_properties = DefineModelProperty(nameof(Properties), new string[] { \\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n_name = DefineProperty(nameof(Name), new string[] { \\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n_location = DefineProperty(nameof(Location), new string[] { \\u0026#34;location\\u0026#34; }, isRequired: true);\\n```\\n\\nSince the Bicep emitter uses insertion order, this causes `properties:` to appear before `name:` in the Bicep output.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nAll regenerated provisioning libraries will have a different Bicep property ordering than before. While functionally equivalent, this is a behavioral change that breaks existing tests and differs from standard Bicep conventions where `name` and `location` typically appear before `properties`.\\n\\n### Proposed fix\\n\\nThe provisioning generator should ensure ARM resource common properties (`name`, `location`, `tags`) are always defined before the `properties` bag and other custom properties in `DefineProvisionableProperties()`.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
